faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my East Meadow home?
faqs.answer: Signs of clothes moth infestation include small irregular holes in wool, silk, or cashmere clothing, thin webbing in closet corners, silken tubes or cases in fabric folds, and small beige or gold-colored moths flying near wardrobes. You may also notice larvae (small cream-colored caterpillars) on fabrics or shedded skin casings in affected areas. Our East Meadow technicians provide thorough inspections to confirm infestations and identify all affected areas.

faqs.question: What does clothes moth control service cost in East Meadow?
faqs.answer: Clothes moth control services in East Meadow typically range from $550 to $5,000 depending on the size of your property, severity of the infestation, number of rooms affected, and treatment methods required. Minor infestations in a single closet or room will be at the lower end, while whole-home treatments for severe, multi-room infestations require more extensive service. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before beginning any treatment.

Our Long Island team is familiar with the area and can quickly reach properties throughout East Meadow and surrounding communities.

faqs.question: What methods do you use for clothes moth control, and are they safe for my family?
faqs.answer: We use integrated pest management combining thorough inspection, targeted insecticide applications to affected areas, pheromone traps for monitoring, heat treatments when appropriate, and detailed prevention recommendations. All products we use are EPA-approved and applied according to strict safety guidelines to protect your family and pets. We also provide guidance on non-chemical prevention methods including proper cleaning, storage techniques, and environmental modifications to prevent future infestations.
